description_long: This bungalow features a traditional Japanese architectural style with a prominent, sweeping hip-and-gable roof adorned with subtle decorative elements. The structure is primarily constructed from wood, with visible wooden beams, posts, and sliding shoji-style windows and doors. The main entrance is marked by a wide porch area, partially covered by the extended roof, and the facade includes a signboard above the central window. The roof displays a gentle curvature and is covered in wooden shingles, with some areas showing natural weathering or moss. The building sits on a raised wooden platform, and the windows are arranged in a balanced, horizontal pattern across the front and sides. The overall appearance is harmonious and serene, evoking the ambiance of a traditional Japanese residence or temple.
description: A traditional Japanese-style wooden bungalow with a sweeping hip-and-gable roof, wide porch, and shoji windows, set on a raised platform.
description_view_0: The front view shows the main entrance, wide porch, central signboard, and symmetrical arrangement of shoji windows under the large, curved roof.
description_view_1: The left side view highlights the depth of the porch, the layered roof structure, and the side shoji doors, with less window detail visible.
description_view_2: The rear view reveals a simpler facade with fewer windows, the full expanse of the roof, and the extended side section of the building.
description_view_3: The right side view displays the smaller gabled roof section, side windows, and the continuation of the porch, emphasizing the building's asymmetry.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical front-facing windows with grouped side windows
